REPLY TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES (TRANSACTION ID # 100050665) FILED BY DEFENDANT YU, DANYI LIN, CHESTER

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS DANYI YU AND CHESTER LIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS' DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1345 REQUIRING A SEPARATE STATEMENT IN SUPPORT OF A MOTION SEEKING FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE FROM THE FACE OF DEFENDANTS' MOVING PAPERS WHETHER OR NOT PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DATE COMPLY WITH HIS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE CODE. NONETHELESS PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL CONCEDES THAT FURTHER SUPPLEMENT TO PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS NECESSARY AND FORTHCOMING. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES SET 1, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET 1, AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS SET 1, ON OR BEFORE NOVEMBER 28, 2018. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF PLAINTIFF'S INCARCERATION MAKE THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS UNJUST. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DARREN MANFRO'S MOTION TO STAY THIS CASE FOR ONE YEAR DUE TO MR. MANFRO'S CURRENT INCARCERATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED BASED ON A FULLER SHOWING OF NEED. MR. MANFRO FAILS TO PROVIDE EVEN THE MOST BASIC INFORMATION SUPPORTING HIS STAY REQUEST. THERE IS NO INFORMATION REGARDING WHERE MR. MANFRO IS INCARCERATED OR THE STATUS OF HIS CRIMINAL CASE. NO TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET IN THIS CASE AND, BASED ON THE MINIMAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE CHARGES AGAINST HIM, IT IS ENTIRELY POSSIBLE THAT MR. MANFRO COULD BE RELEASED FROM CUSTODY WELL BEFORE TRIAL. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-20-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Plaintiff Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Danyi Yu and Chester Lin's motion to compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendants' Discovery Requests and Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants have failed to comply with CRC 3.1345 requiring a Separate Statement in support of a motion seeking further discovery responses and the Court is unable to determine from the face of Defendants' moving papers whether or not Plaintiff's responses to date comply with his obligations under the Code. Nonetheless Plaintiff's opposition to the motion to compel concedes that further supplement to Plaintiff's discovery responses is necessary and forthcoming. Plaintiff shall provide supplemental responses to form interrogatories set 1, special interrogatories set 1, and requests for production of documents set 1, on or before November 28, 2018. The request for monetary sanctions is denied because the circumstances of Plaintiff's incarceration make the imposition of sanctions unjust.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Set For Trial On Apr-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Stay The Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nikolaus W. Reed, Esq. of Law Office Nikolaus W. Reed, 415.940.7766, appearing for Plaintiff;
Patricia M. Green Roe or Samuelson Wilson & Roe, 408.673.9594, appearing for Defendant.

PLAINTIFF DARREN MANFRO'S NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS is ARGUED.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, Plaintiff Darren Manfro's motion to stay this case for one year due to Mr. Manfro's current incarceration is denied without prejudice to being refiled based on a fuller showing of need. Mr. Manfro fails to provide even the most basic information supporting his stay request. There is no information regarding where Mr. Manfro is incarcerated or the status of his criminal case. No trial date has been set in this case and, based on the minimal information about the charges against him, it is entirely possible that Mr. Manfro could be released from custody well before trial. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
